title: Nimzowitsch Defense: Classical
summary: Learn the Nimzowitsch Defense, a hypermodern opening where Black puts a knight on the fourth rank early.
description: The Nimzowitsch is an aggressive, slightly unsound opening where Black moves a knight to c6 or b4 early. You'll learn why it's dangerous if White doesn't respond accurately.
line: 1.e4 Nc6 2.Nf3 e6 3.d4 d5 4.Bb5 Nf6 5.e5 Nd7 6.Bxc6 bxc6
objectives: Understand the Nimzowitsch idea: unconventional development for dynamic play.
Learn typical White responses: develop and punish lazy knights.
Master the key: don't let Black develop in peace.
plans: White uses the extra move to develop and attack the knight.
Black seeks to justify the early knight with piece activity.
The position is sharp: accurate play decides.
commonMistakes: Do not over-react. The knight is not dangerous if you develop.
Avoid chasing the knight. Just develop and punish.
completionSummary: The Nimzowitsch Defense teaches that unusual development can be punished with accurate play.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.instruction: Play e4 to control central space.
steps.coaching: White controls the center. Black has a surprise coming.
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: The Nimzowitsch Defense
steps.instruction: The knight develops to c6, an unusual but aggressive move.
steps.coaching: This is Nimzowitsch: Black moves the knight to c6, attacking e5.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nf3, developing and attacking e5.
steps.coaching: Natural development. White attacks the knight.

steps.title: Black solidifies
steps.instruction: Play e6, supporting d5 and preparing development.
steps.coaching: Black solidifies the center. The position is taking shape.

steps.title: Claim central space
steps.instruction: Play d4 to control the center.
steps.coaching: White builds central control. Black must develop carefully.

steps.title: Black challenges the center
steps.coaching: Black challenges. The center is now contested.

steps.title: Attack the knight
steps.instruction: Move Bb5, attacking the knight on c6.
steps.coaching: White attacks the knight. Black must decide: move or defend.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.coaching: Black develops. The position is complex.

steps.title: Push in the center
steps.coaching: White gains space. The knight on c6 is now under pressure.

steps.title: Black moves the knight
steps.coaching: Black moves the knight to safety. The position is complex.

steps.title: Trade the bishop
steps.instruction: Capture on c6, winning a pawn.
steps.coaching: White wins a pawn. But Black gets counterplay.

steps.title: Black recaptures
steps.coaching: Black gets a weak pawn but open lines. The game is complex.
